Off Calendar May-02-2025 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Continued To May-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-28-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion (Motions)
Off Calendar Apr-19-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for application for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:33:11 a.m. and concluding at 10:49:35 a.m.

Plaintiff Starkdrive LLC represented by David S. Ratner, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Christian Adel Michael not appearing.

OSCAR STARK is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

This matter was continued for Plaintiff to show the actual donation amount made to INDIEGOGO.
Supplemental Declaration of Oscar Stark in Support of Request for Entry of Default Judgment filed on 03/21/2023. The Court has reviewed the case and all filings pertaining to this hearing.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit A - 2 Screenshots Confirming Donation to INDIEGOGO

OSCAR STARK is examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the testimony of Plaintiff's witness, evidence presented, application for entry of default judgment, and additional exhibits, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint. Defendant was properly served. Statement of Damages was served via US Mail. Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence of the donation to Defendant in the sum of $285,062.00 as descibed in Paragraph 46 of its Complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Starkdrive LLC, and against Defendants Christian Adel Michael and Mate.Bike International IVS, as follows:

Plaintiff waives attorney fees.
Plaintiff's request for punitive damages is DENIED.
Court awards interest at the rate of 10% of damages from 02/23/2017 thru 04/19/2023. Counsel for Plaintiff to calculate interest.

1. Damages: $285,062.00

Counsel for Plaintiff to calculate interest and submit proposed judgment.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-27-2023 Continued To May-23-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion (Motions)
Off Calendar Feb-07-2023 Continued To Apr-19-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-09-2023 Continued To Mar-21-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion (Motions)
Off Calendar Dec-13-2022 Continued To Feb-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default prove-up hearing and entry of judgment.

Dept. 514; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:30:31 a.m. and concluding at 11:32:30 a.m.

Plaintiff Star Drive, LLC represented by David S. Ratner, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Christian Adel Michael not appearing.
Defendant Mate.Bike International IVS not appearing.

OSCAR STARK is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court examines Plaintiff's Counsel re: service on Defendants.
Service issue is resolved by Exhibit B to Declaration of David S. Ratner in Support of Motion for Default Prove-Up Hearing and Entry of Judgment filed Aug-18-2022.

Based on Plaintiff's Statement of Damages filed May-27-2022, the Court determines that Plaintiff seeks the folowing:

1. Damages: $285,062.00
2. Punitive Damages: $1,000,000.00
3. Interest (as of today): $165,336.00
4. Attorney Fees: WAIVED

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.
OSCAR STARK (Chief Executive Officer) is examined by the Court based on its personal knowledge and declaration. Witness attests that statements by its counsel are true and correct.

Court cannot determine damages.
Counsel for Plaintiff shall file a supplemental declaration with evidence to support witnesses' statement of damages and demonstrate the basis for its request for $285,062.00 in damages. A Courtesy copy shall be delivered to Department 514 prior to the next hearing.

Court orders matter continued to Feb-08-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 to address damages. Appearances via Zoom Video Conference are permitted.
